meta.title: Viridian Prospects
meta.summary: Authored quest scenes for Prospector Helga's mining and steelmaking line in the Viridian Range.

config.title: Prospector Helga and the Upper Veins
config.idleText: A mountain with ore is still just a mountain if no one can work it. If I have nothing for you, then either the claim is holding or the next problem hasn't climbed high enough yet.

available.promptText: These mountains are rich enough to make fools brave and corpses expensive. If you're interested in a partnership, start by learning the difference between a vein and a trap.
available.acceptedText: Good. Then understand this first: prospecting is logistics disguised as greed.
available.detailsText: I don't worship ore. I plan around what it costs to extract, defend, refine, and move. That's why my stakes still stand.
available.freeformText: Ask. Partnerships survive better on clear expectations than charm.
available.freeformPrompt: Ask Helga what you should understand first about her claim.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What's the real problem up here besides the ore?
available.freeformFallback: Helga taps a rock face with her hammer. "Ask one useful thing."

stages.completedText: Helga has laid out the gamble. Now she wants proof you can make the mountain pay.

available.promptText: Iron Ore from the upper veins is the backbone of every army worth naming. Bring me five and prove the claim still beats the beasts.
available.acceptedText: Five Iron Ore. Bring me the kind of haul that justifies the climb.
available.detailsText: Because iron is infrastructure. Fancy ore impresses merchants; iron keeps civilization from forgetting how to equip itself.
available.freeformText: Then ask. Good miners think about the route out before the first swing in.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Helga what you need before mining the upper veins.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What makes the upper veins worth the risk?
available.freeformFallback: Helga squints at you. "Ask about the haul or go get it."

active.promptText: The ore run isn't complete. Bring back the Iron Ore and then we can talk about why the camps keep getting wrecked.
active.resultText: Good. That keeps the claim honest. Unfortunately, ore attracts attention as reliably as blood does.
active.secondaryText: Five Iron Ore from the upper veins before the claim loses the route.

stages.completedText: The ore haul is done. Helga's next problem has fur and terrible timing.

available.promptText: Mountain Yetis wrecked my last two camps because they understand weak infrastructure when they see it. I want them taught otherwise.
available.acceptedText: Three Mountain Yetis. Remind them the camps are not up for adoption.
available.detailsLabel: Why do the Yetis matter to the claim?
available.detailsText: Because camps are capital in physical form. Lose the camp and every future haul gets more expensive before it even starts.
available.freeformText: Ask. Camp defense is accounting with claws in it.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Helga what you need before dealing with the Yetis.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What part of the camp do they ruin first?
available.freeformFallback: Helga snorts. "Ask clearly or go teach them."

active.promptText: The Yetis are still taking liberties with my equipment. Finish the clear-out.
active.resultText: Good. Now the claim can breathe long enough for the only part I actually enjoy: turning ore into something worth selling.
active.secondaryText: Three Mountain Yetis threatening Helga's camps and route security.
active.declineText: Claims left undefended become stories told by other people.

stages.completedText: The camp pressure is reduced. Helga's last test is whether you can turn raw gain into actual value.

available.promptText: Good ore means nothing if it never becomes steel. Forge the alloys and prove you understand the difference between a haul and a business.
available.acceptedText: Two Steel Alloys. Quality work only, or don't bother bringing it back.
available.detailsLabel: Why end on steel instead of more mining?
available.detailsText: Because extraction is only the first half of a claim's intelligence. Refinement is where value stops being theoretical.
available.freeformText: Then ask. Final work should sound like standards, not enthusiasm.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Helga what you need before forging the Steel Alloys.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What separates acceptable steel from good steel here?
available.freeformFallback: Helga folds her arms. "Ask one proper smithing question, or get to the forge."

active.promptText: The forge work still isn't done. Bring me the Steel Alloys and then we'll call this partnership productive.
active.resultText: Good. That's the difference between scavenging and industry. You might actually be worth the split.
active.secondaryText: Two Steel Alloys forged well enough to justify the extraction effort behind them.
active.declineText: Ore sitting in a sack is not profit.

stages.completedText: Helga has already judged the steel fit. The claim is in better hands than it was.
